{"key_event":"Late Qing self-strengthening under foreign pressure","reasoning":"The Qing state retained a large bureaucracy, tax apparatus, and imperial legitimacy, but its fiscal-military capacity and territorial control were weakened by foreign encroachment, rebellions' aftermath, and dependence on provincial officials. Civil society had lineage, guild, gentry, and local charitable networks with some capacity for local coordination, but little autonomous ability to hold the imperial state accountable or mobilize nationally.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":3.5,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":5.0}
